identitySentence: The Law Offices of Nathan Miller, PLLC is a Denton criminal defense firm founded in 2010 by Nathan Miller, a former Denton County prosecutor who has practiced law since 2008, defending people facing DWI, drug, theft, assault, and other criminal charges in Denton County.

concept.headline: Arrested in Denton County? Put a Former Denton County Prosecutor in Your Corner
concept.subheadline: After an arrest, the uncertainty is the hardest part: what happens next, what it means for your record, and who you can trust to fight for you. Tell The Law Offices of Nathan Miller, PLLC what you are facing and get a response with clear next steps and real consultation times.
credibilityPoints: Nathan Miller has practiced law since 2008 and founded the firm in 2010
Former Denton County prosecutor now defending the accused
Super Lawyers 2025 selection with an Avvo 10.0 rating
Published case results of firm-reported dismissals and charge reductions

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: The firm defends people charged with DWI, drug crimes, theft, assault and family violence, sex crimes, and weapons offenses. It also handles record sealing, DWI record sealing, and pretrial diversion, with a practice built around criminal trial work.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: The firm offices at 1701 N. Locust St in Denton, and Nathan Miller served as a Denton County prosecutor before founding the firm. A second office in Southlake serves Grapevine, Colleyville, Keller, Westlake, and Trophy Club.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: Your inquiry goes straight to the firm's inbox and you receive a reply specific to your situation along with available consultation times. Your appointment is confirmed with a $50 reservation deposit, and the firm will ask about the charge, the timeline, and what outcome matters most to you.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

To the team at The Law Offices of Nathan Miller, PLLC:

Mr. Miller spent part of his career on the other side of the courtroom as a Denton County prosecutor before founding your firm in 2010, and your published case results show what that perspective is worth: firm-reported dismissals in assault, felony theft, and juvenile robbery cases, and DWI charges reduced or fought to a hung jury.

We built one product, called the Lead Capturer, and we built a version of it specifically for your firm. We would like you to test it before deciding anything.

Why a Lead Capturer fits your practice

Criminal defense inquiries rarely arrive at convenient times. An arrest, a DWI stop, a family violence accusation: these happen on nights and weekends, and the person searching for help is often contacting several firms at once. Your website already does more than most, with intake forms throughout, online scheduling, and a strong case results page. What no website can do on its own is answer back immediately, in writing, with a reply specific to the caller's situation, and then turn that reply into a confirmed appointment. That is the entire job of your Lead Capturer.

What we built for you

Your Lead Capturer carries a custom identity sentence and a custom contact form shaped around your practice areas: DWI, drug crimes, theft, assault and family violence, sex crimes, weapons charges, record sealing, and pretrial diversion. Your identity sentence reads:

"The Law Offices of Nathan Miller, PLLC is a Denton criminal defense firm founded in 2010 by Nathan Miller, a former Denton County prosecutor who has practiced law since 2008, defending people facing DWI, drug, theft, assault, and other criminal charges in Denton County."

How it works

Every inquiry submitted through your form is emailed directly to your firm's inbox. The potential client automatically receives a response specific to the topic of their message, so a DWI inquiry gets a DWI-appropriate reply and a record-sealing inquiry gets a record-sealing reply. That response presents your available consultation times, and a $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so the consultations on your calendar belong to people committed to showing up.

The 15-day proof period

We are not asking you to buy anything on faith. We will run your complete Lead Capturer for 15 days so you can test every part of the experience yourself: the inquiry email, the automatic response, the scheduling, and the deposit. We make no revenue promises. The only claim we make is that the system works, and the test below lets you verify that claim yourself.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email address you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information.
3. Confirm your firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject line identifies your firm and the likely matter type.
6. Confirm the Reply-To address contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to your TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm the response is sent to the customer email without any staff action.
9. Confirm the customer email receives it.
10. Confirm it addresses the actual inquiry topic.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails — contact us.

Reply to this email and we will send your private test link the same day.

The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
